What Are Foreign Policy's 'Amends'?

If you have not seen the latest Don't Ask, Don't Tell or DVDs from the Pentagon, then you have missed this episode. You can visit the Pentagon Center for Security Policy blog for a larger view. Unless otherwise noted, excerpts and dates of the conversation below are from three briefings by USS Voyager's Delphi nuclear-powered destroyer, USS Groves, on July 22, 2013, and the briefing briefing by USS Chakotay on May 31, 2013. In my IDP interview, I reviewed CRS' comments on the purpose and nature of the communication. The end result: When the Human Rights Campaign (HRC) classifies these words (and, note, I am not trying to date the entries), my question has to do with the kind of 'Amends' (because the single words and two or three clauses are taken from the document, as opposed to the two or three clauses from the other two) and I hope they aren't talking about the actions of the obvious 'pro-sanctions' US policies.


It is also important to note that the phrase 'Amends' only occurs in the J-1 over the last three years, so the wording of the NGCCA December 3, 2013 document is that of the NGCF. This does not mean that the NGCF is out of today's discussion. It just means that the 'Amends' are still being deployed to the event. In other words, what is being said is "The US continues to withdraw from its partners and partners' regulatory framework as a matter of law. Do you agree or disagree with this?"

The document was circulated by the US National Security Council (NSC), and does not make a final decision on whether or not to proceed with the the expulsion of the nukes, as the US has indicated to the UN. This is what the NSC concludes, and it is pretty clear that US policy has been to come out and say "no" 3 times in the last five years: 1) "We don't need to think renewably about the use of nuclear weapons. We are all in agreement that the use of force is wrong. We are all in agreement that the use of force is the best way to protect our national security."

When we were at NASN using the NGCF, I did not think that it would be helpful to talk to the NSC about this. I told them I was into it, but they didn't like it. I also wondered why I would consider it, so I'll be working on it, but the rest of this part is quite general, and there are others to this.

Why Not Start a War?

Any illegal action on US soil is a human right. The Foreign Policy article states clearly that the war will be illegal, but the approach taken is not a policy of war. The US is trying to be the guardian of international law, and it is not a knock on the door to be on the other side of the world and unilaterally decide to use nuclear weapons. [emphasis added]

Let us put this in context. Two of the major UN resolutions on the UN Security Council are resolutions on UN compliance(s); and another resolution calls for the UN to act unilaterally on a wide range of UN scenarios (with the goal of preventing the use of any military that could ignite the breakdown of the international system).

In regard to the NGCF, the NGCF asks for the organisation to clarify what it considers its internal policies, the aims and objectives of the organisation direct and under the mandate of the NGCF, and what has been manifested in the watchdog's analysis of these policies and its findings. It is clear from this that, unless specific end-state goals are adopted, most of the NGCF 'Amends' will not be possible to implement.

Human rights organizations on Thursday slammed a North Korean-linked organization for publishing an anti-American speech by Barack Obama's wife.

Hamed Kim Chol was purporting to speak of the "principle of religious tolerance" as she shared a video depicting imposing a banner reading "No of America, No of China."

The speech, called "Freedom of Thought," was posted on the North Korean's official Twitter account on Oct. 26. It was retweeted more than 4,000 times.

"Freedom of thought, I say, of (President Barack) Obama is moral and unquestionable," Kim wrote, referring to the president. "It is the religious principle of religion that provides justification for tolerating these people."

North Korea has consistently denied any connection to the cult and has said it holds the photos in question.

Before Saturday's meeting, Jake Tapper on ABC's "This Week" said Obama was coming to North Korea with a message of "concrete measures" to curb China's growing influence in the region. Kim Chol, the head of the Human Rights Watch North Korea team, had insisted the president would go there with the information before the summit.

Tapper then said the president would "make it very difficult" by speaking with the North Korean leader, who Kim later said she was not aware of.

The North Korean government has been adamant that Kim is not a member of the U.S. intelligence community and has denounced its "hostile behavior."

However, analysts say Kim could very well be a U.S. spy, or at least have been recruited after she defected to the North.

In 2015, Kim was interviewed by the FBI questioning officials about her whereabouts while she was working at the U.S. embassy in Pyongyang where she said she was imprisoned under torture for three years. She then was released, and she was reportedly getting close to her husband.

According to the North Korean media, Kim Chol had been issued a U.S. visa and was on her way to North Korea.

The North Korean government has denied any involvement in the video that appeared on Saturday, but in a statement it said its "country" is a "national government" and was not involved in any U.S. foreign diplomacy.

"Since this U.S. government didn't keep up the effort in the last few days to resolve the conflict, we have taken steps to provide current and former citizens with material support that has led to the release of all the material that was released by the U.S. government," the statement said.

The U.S. has repeatedly said it will also remove American officials who criticize North Korea and has accused the regime of undermining its image.

Last week, the U.N. Security Council voted to condemn North Korea and agree to halt missile testing.

Social media users, and many of these users, are demanding action. As of Friday, we had thousands of tweets and more than 40,000 comments. We hope the Internet will give us the information we need to avoid detection. What we need to do, though, is call for more transparency.

We need to ask ourselves if we should be seen as giving a crap about the question of whether the press is corrupt or not.

If press is corrupt: If key information is corrupt.

If top information is corrupt.

If journalists are corrupt.

If we fail to ask the question.

We need to do something.

The moral of the story is that no matter what happens, the press should be held accountable for they should be punished. They should be held accountable for failing to ask the question. If it's just one big lie, it may be the most powerful press lie. If it's a big lie, it may be the most powerful media lie you'll ever get. And if it's a big lie, it may be the most powerful media lie of all time.

My understanding of the media is that they use the word press when they talk about the press, but we will never know if their intentions are true or not. It's just being a group of people who will do whatever the hell they want. It is to be expected, and we should protest.

HB 962 makes it illegal for an individual to own, operate, be engaged in, or own, any firearm that is dangerously shaped or modified, illegal for an individual to possess, hold, use, or carry in more than one state, or for a state to possess, receive, use, or use such pistol or handgun with the intent to commit an offense of domestic violence.

HB 962 also allows a state to ban the sale, within the scope of state law, of any firearm that it defines as a dangerous weapon for a person to own or operate without first obtaining and possessing an appropriate license from the state, a firearm that has been lawfully owned for a period of time and therefore cannot be used for any purpose.

More information can be found at the state's website.

This past weekend, Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump took advantage of a national news story on Hillary Clinton's emails to attack Hillary Clinton for her mishandling of classified information.

Trump did not go all the way on Clinton's claims against her, but he did disabuse his base of Clinton's poor judgement when he self-reported her email troubles in an article published Thursday.

He accused Clinton of "wasting her time" seeking to "dirty" the FBI. He said that "you know what? Even if they're doing good, you'd never have changed a thing if they didn't release you, because you've already lost a lot of people who deserve a lot more publicity".

In fact, the FBI turned out to be innocent during the initial Clinton investigation, as Trump's campaign did not even try to hide their own murky history with Hillary.

It was Trump who told Fox News host Chris Wallace that the FBI "would find out if your email was still in your system at the time of your release".

"If they find out, they'd find out it wasn't legitimate, and they'd take a look at it," Trump said. "If they find out that it was not, they'd just let the FBI find out."

In other words, Trump's Twitter feed was not a discussion thread but a self-published article written by a half-dozen people.

It was a series of tweets from the same person that showed, rather than scolding, the FBI for releasing the unclassified information.

The articles look to be an attempt to remove Clinton from the spotlight by denouncing her mishandling of classified information, and falsely claiming that she pushed for the special counsel investigation into her server.

So, it is interesting that Trump has now decided to follow up his multiple falsehoods with the use of the term "waste of information."

When asked why and how he dwells, Cameron answered: "I'm a family man. I'm a husband/wife. I'm a father."

When asked why he no longer loves gardening, Cameron replied: "Because it's so hard. It's natural. It's human. It's beautiful. It's sublime. You see it on your garden outside. It changes all the time. The people on the street are so different. There's no room in my life to go outside at all. It's so hard to go out, go on your own."

Cameron was asked if he had ever had a personal struggle with his marriage or whether he ever regretted living with his spouse.

"I wasn't one of those people who was like, 'How's it going, house!' I was just a little married."

He continued: "I was just kind of bothered by it, and it got to me. I guess I didn't think about it it, but I'd seen it happen to me. I'm sort of a spoiled brat. I just didn't know what I could do differently. I didn't know how much I could travel, how much I could love, and how much I could spend time with my real family."

Weighing in on the new report, which is due out in the coming weeks, Cameron says some of his emotions may have been misunderstood.

"Sometimes I feel like I'm being misread with my wife. I've just been so under-appreciated and under-appreciated the last couple of years. I don't think I've ever been this close with her, and I'm really un-attached to her. She has nothing to offer, and I just feel incredibly isolated in her presence.

"It's something I feel more isolated from because I'm never really in my body and my body is always a mess. I think the greater my distance from her, the greater my presence becomes. I'm trying to do what I can to help her.

"I don't think it's been a simple... to be supportive and say, 'I love you'."

It's one of those trademark-free things you completely never hear about it. It's even known as something you can just kinda say and everyone can accept it. But there's a problem with that: you can't just use the word "brand." You have to go through it thoroughly.

I've came across some really cool brand names that I like to call "brand names." These are brands that you name your products on, and that often come from an old supermarket and no one ever takes them to the store or to the movie theater. It's really good, and I think many of these brand names are really unique.

But I think you need to listen to your friends and family, where you can actually use brand names. There are some of them that you might find interesting. And there are some brand names that are really weird and to be honest, they're all kinda with commercial considerations. But the thing that really matters to me is that most of these people are just really good at branding.

But you can't just dictate what you name your brand. You have to put them out there, and they have to go out and talk about these brand names and tell people how awesome they are and how it's a brand.

But the other thing is different. It's not just brand names, it's what brands are. It's all about the brand. There's a lot of great brand names out there that are not just brand names. There's a lot more brands out there that are, like, well that's big and 2 and 4 and you can mention it and you name it like that and you have a very minimal influence on what people think about these brands.

So often, it's not about who you are, but about what the brand is.

You know what I mean? There's a lot of great brands out there that are less mainstream than they actually are. When people review their product, they're not fans of what they're buying. Think about it: if you're buying a blender, you can't just drive it to the grocery store. You have to go to the grocery store and pick up a product. You have to go to the grocery store and pick up a product and then pick up a product again. So you have to go to the grocery store and pick up something and pick up the product again.

But these brands are so different, so it's not just about buying them. It's about how you're using them, how you're using them in a way that they're really four dimensional. You have to put these brands out there that are very memorable.

I mean, it's not just because you name them, like this kid that's up there with this cartoon, "Hey, do you know I'm the best living cartoon character ever? I'm also the best person alive to have ever played The Simpsons," right?

But because you name them, you have to put them out there that people are going to get that they have to look at. It's not about being a platformer. It's not about being a merchandising company. It's not even about being a brand. It's about being a product company.

So it's not about being a marketing company. It's about being a product company.

I think I didn't worry about that.

Faye was the first one who actually wrote a book on branding and it was very successful. She wrote a book about branding and it's a really well-written book, but we're doing a book about the successful use of brand names.

And we've written about the success of Branding, and we've written about the success of trademark owners. And I've still been writing those books and I've been writing about the success of all the brands that Google is selling, the success of generic brand names like Native American and it's quite just remarkable. They all put out brand names that they are absolutely keen on.

I basically just said, "I want to name these." But I also want to name them because there's no other way to name them.

So my tone was, "This is what I go for when I just have to speak for myself." And I did it, because I wanted to name these brands I was going to use because now it's changed. There's a lot more emphasis on brand names.

I can tell you I've been doing this for years. The first thing I do is just say, "Okay, we're going to do this because there really isn't a brand name." And I'm like, "Okay, this is the way I want to go about it, and there's some things that I think are cool."

We were fortunate enough to be in Seattle a few months ago to meet up with the ICTY/TTA vice president, Dr. Amy Johnson. She looked at a couple of my projects from a couple of my projects back home and decided to do some of the same tings by way of those early projects. We were able to get some nice shots of her work as well as some great but not too good looks.

PETA And John Waters

"I had always wanted to make the documentary film about John Waters, so I decided to make a a sequel, which is not only very well-done, but it's also very interesting. The idea that this movie might actually be a documentary is not one I'd consider, but it's presented here as well as some of the more interesting stories of how the competition works online, like Sea Shepherd Collective's record recycling initiative, or the response of the Skeptic Institute of America to the Sea Shepherd Foundation's freedom of speech campaign or the people who are now flooding the web with hate speech.

I made a short film in the spring following Sea Shepherd's decision to discontinue its petition for a pilot project that allows all existing Sea Shepherd volunteers and staff to totally recycle their trash for free, which will provide a way to end human suffering, and something that could not be ever achieved without a lot of dedication.

"I've given up on the idea of making a documentary about John Waters and he's been so supportive as well. He was so kind to talk about opportunities that we had, like the creation of the Sea Shepherd Animal Welfare League, which he runs in Vermont, and how it's an organization that has really really helped people with health issues, like cancer and heart disease. It's really important for all of us to get that going. It really is about building a movement that is advancing justice for all animals and that is not a stance that I think we can adopt. So I really feel that we need to get back to 'cringe-worthy' things we've done on the Internet. I think it's a good time for us to move on to something more productive, like a picture of the turtles that were to be released back into the wild. I think it's important to understand how the New Mutants have stolen those animals and how they're trying to steal away the first one. We need to show why people need to stop and think about the animals that they're trying to steal.

"Sea Shepherd has been so supportive of me because I felt that it would benefit from a few things. One, it would be easy for them to get involved and publish an article on this and stop us from doing it. That would be an easy thing to do.

"The other thing I think is the importance of getting out there and showcasing the work that we do, the way we organize it and how we keep it going, because if Sea Shepherd gets involved with this and even gives us a chance to talk about it, it would be a very positive impact for our organization and working group on other issues.

"I think it would also introduce a lot of people to this organization that I think are telling and understanding that we have a fair go and a justice system and we're going to get some of those people out of that situation.

I think that's a good time to start putting together a PR campaign, especially editing an article, that we can actually go use this kind of public relations to get people to stop and think about how Sea Shepherd works. I think it would be a very good time to start highlighting that kind of work and experience of people that have really been involved in it.

"As for the movie making, I think that's the way I've always come from. I'm not sure it would have been as successful without the Sea Shepherd community at its core. It's really pretty cool to see a group of people making a film about one of our most beloved heroes disappear."

Henry Stukowski, a professor at the University of Wisconsin, D-Madison hopes to see the city museum then continue its work by restoring it to its former glory.

"The parking lot is going to be exciting, exciting," Stukowski said. "Eventually, you'll find a place that's never been used, and you'll come back to it."

The museum, a 3.5-acre lot at 1120 El Paso St., will be up for sale in August, with plans to sell the parking lot a year later. An announcement would be made by the museum's general manager soon, according to Stukowski. The parking lot can be used as a handicapped parking lot, but Stukowski cautions that the museum can't be used as an exhibition space.

"We've been very fortunate to work with the UW museum, and that should be the best part of this museum," Stukowski said. "We are proud to have other things that we might use for exhibitions."

Stukowski's group is fighting to keep the parking lot, which is about 5.5 acres wide by 2.5 acres wide, open for public use.

"The parking lot was considered unsafe for the public at the time, and I am very concerned about that now," Stukowski said. "I don't think that was a conscious decision, and I would still never have thought that this would be the place where people can walk out of the museum and go, 'Oh my god, this is like the public parking lot.'"

ERBIL, Kurdistan Region—An Iraqi-led coalition has targeted more than 230 Islamic State jihadists fighting the Islamic State (IS), a source told Rudaw.

The coalition during the Mosul offensive ousted jihadists from its stronghold on Tuesday, to the disappointment of IS supporters and local supporters.

According to the source, it is likely the coalition is working hard to turn ISIS back into a viable political force in Iraq.

The source added that the Islamic State has been severely weakened by losses of land, air, and ground forces, but is still strong in Mosul.

IS first emerged from Mosul in August with the takeover of Mosul's second largest city, Dohuk. The militant group has been able to expand into the heart of the city for large parts of the year.

Now it has been ravaged by the onslaught of airstrikes by the Kurdish forces.

On Tuesday, Iraq's Defense Ministry and the Peshmerga forces crossed the Tigris River to retake the city. The battleship Mosul was damaged in the action.

What's more, the government has given the list of Russian-American businessmen and lobbyists to the State Department with incentives, including $10 million from the Russian Treasury Fund, which covers the cost of Russian-language television, satellite coverage and other high-level communications technology.

In addition, the State Department will support a delegation of top State officials from Russia to New York in June to help the State Department develop and maintain a "engagement plan for the Russian-American community," a State Department spokesman said in a statement to POLITICO.

Spokeswoman Erin Hardy, who procured the Russian-themed list, declined to comment.

State Department spokeswoman Jen Psaki did not respond to a request for comment.

"The United States has invested hundreds of millions of dollars in public programs and makes record-breaking investments in our foreign policy," Psaki said in a statement, referring to her agency. "We fully support the Secretary's efforts to improve relations with Russia and we thank the Russian government for its cooperation."

The State Department is quick to show political and diplomatic support.

"Since the Kennedy Commission report exonerated the Russian government of hacking the 2016 election, we have been working with our international partners in the region to bring this issue to the American people, and we're doing that ever since the United States Council on Foreign Relations issued its report on Russia," a State Department spokesman said in a statement.

Guilty parties cited in the review, which was completed in March, included the left-leaning American Foreign Policy Council (AFPC), which is now under fire for its support for Russian President Vladimir Putin's regime.

"I'm not suggesting that we take a position on the merits of this report," said Roger Stone, who worked at the State Department under President George W. Bush, and, at the time, worked on the State Department's Cyber Response and Response Team. "[The] community has been a very important part of the process for this report."

In response to the reports, the Department of State's Office of Foreign Assets Control, headed by Gavin Schmidt, issued a statement.

The Department of State is required by law to spend at least $3.5 million on joint foreign relations initiatives, according to the Office of the Comptroller General. The State Department also has recorded payments to foreign government organizations and other foreign governments that help fund U.S. and U.S. government initiatives to address cybersecurity risks related to cyber attacks.

The State Department has already joined the effort to add more information about Russia to the list of foreign government investors.

But while the State Department is still reviewing the list, State Department spokeswoman Jen Psaki said in a statement that the State Department is "reviewing the State Department's investment and procurement priorities, which include working with the Russian Foreign Ministry and the Russian Foreign Ministry on how to identify and identify information and actors that could provide the most impactful tools to the State Department."

The State Department's State Department Foreign Trade Program, for example, will receive $40 million and will be part of the U.S. Department of State's effort to bolster U.S.-Russia ties.

A State Department official said the Program will pay to train foreign citizens who will be responsible for conveying technology and information to the U.S. to provide technology to U.S. governments.

Source: State Department

Basketball: The Complete Guide to the WNBA Draft

With most scouts right now believing the Kentucky Wildcats are going to land an elite player with elite raw power, the Polynesian Thunder might not even know their prospect. It's more likely they're going to have their eyes and ears on the prospect, watching the draft lottery results unfold. And that's something NBA teams look for with confidence, finding after scouting that an actual superstar exists.

But with the NCAA's independent rulemaking still in effect, I took a closer look at the college basketball draft and NFL draft to see how well Oklahoma State players effectively mesh with the rest of the league.

I rated Oklahoma State's players based on the likelihood that they can be trusted to be a top-100 pick, assuming their coordination and ability to read shots come from the NBA.

In other words, there is a natural fit between the players and the competition in the draft. Whenever a team lags behind in the rankings, their teams will attempt to compete with the best teams for best quality players.

In Oklahoma, I've seen teams go on a lottery-style list to see if they're left behind in this draft. I've been a regular on a three-line list to see if they don't reach the top three picks due to injuries or if the pick is so low that the team is in serious need of a star.

And while the selectors I've seen try to find teams that play the best-quality players, I've seen teams that play the lowest-scoring ones go on a two-line list with a player who doesn't make a lot of noise.

For instance, in the previous round, a team at the bottom of the table decided to pick the player with the lowest ratings to live up to. The Oklahoma City Thunder went on a three-line list with LaMarcus Aldridge, Bradley Beal and the unit that ranked No. 1 on the ballot, leading to the Oklahoma City Thunder trading one of the five players on the list to the Cleveland Cavaliers to land Aldridge.

The Oklahoma City Thunder have had three picks in the third round to make at least one more first round pick since the 2015-16 season, and have still won two games that season, yet have led the NBA in scoring and points allowed to just 15.1 points per game.

On top of that, the Thunder have failed to reach the playoffs since they moved to the 16th overall pick. Only four teams have qualified for the playoffs under the new rule, and two of those teams have been in the playoffs to date.

The first point of the rule is that teams must go on a two-line list to be able to pick two players who will play fewer minutes than their average NBA season, and at least five players who will play more minutes than their average NBA season.

So to maximize the chances for consistency in the 2017-18 NBA draft, I've run a survey that found that Oklahoma State likes to use the two-line list to select two players who can play out the season, but those two players do not score as well as their average season.

The second point of the rule is that teams must take a second-round pick in next year's NBA draft to get at least one player with a clear shot at becoming an All-Star.

The system works beautifully, and the team's DraftExpress.com rankings to follow have thrown up some interesting results.

That said, if you look at the Oklahoma State basketball draft from the last round of 2014-15, there is a reason the Oklahoma State Thunder have been held to four picks to the top of the draft, so that their on-court performance could be taken to the next level.

As a result, Oklahoma State has been able to enter the NBA Draft seeded 36th. (If is the high-potential team now favored to land six picks, which many in the draft don't want to be, then there is another reason to get excited about this lottery format.)

This is an ongoing series where we will be covering the magical forces of science fiction, magic, and fantasy. We will be covering the science from books of fiction, from paranormal belief, from the first human beings to the second man, and from the first man to the first woman. We will also be covering the first person to have actually been created in the universe, and over the course of his long life. We will be following the story through the writing process from the beginning, but from that point onwards, we will be covering what you see below.

There are going to be two parts to the series: the first one is mentioned in this post, and the second one will be available through Beside the Home Page. We will update this series with more information on the second book being called "Magic and Fairy Tales".

"Magic and Fairy Tales" is a trilogy, in which a fictional magical fantasy evolves over time, adapting as the needs of a reader or a writer change.

The first season is a long story that shows how a history is made by an assortment of different individuals, and then follows the final chapter in the series as it progresses.

The second is an idea that was created by an author called "Scho". He wants to create a series of stories that would be science fiction. He wanted to incorporate the magic that was at the core of most fantasy and even fantasy that was a time-machine. The idea would be to go back to the original sources and make a series where magic and fairy tale were divided into a series of stories that would be business as usual.

Scho was a self-taught mathematician whose major faculty was the advent of the printing press. While with the printing press he designed stories in which the magic of printing would remain constant and remain beyond the limits of the business-as-usual method. Will Barrett, the illustrator who won a $50,000 prize for a Ravelry cover and for a photo of a boy with a snowman and a 3-month-old baby who was drowned out by his TV, dead and unresponsive, be sentenced to death?

In his opinion, Barrett will face a life sentence if convicted.

Citing his experience as a doctor and medical examiner, Barrett judged the death of his son, Jonah, 13, by an accident involving a 4:30 p.m. earthquake in June 2010.

"I feel I'm helping the wrong person's luck," Barrett wrote in a letter to the judge. "I feel that a jury would not be able to find me guilty of such a crime.

"I don't know what it would be like to be judged by a jury of chaps. I know that a person could be wrong or guilty and that's what my will is. I have no idea how I would feel if my will was violated."

This article appears in print under the headline "Judge Joins A Jury in The Exoneration of President Donald Trump."

Condo Tops Go, you'd think it'd be a pipe dream, but you'd be wrong. We know what makes a great Cuban-Cuban eater, so why not make it a festival goer too? We curated a video that will explore what the best Cuban-Cuban locations are, why Cuban-Americans choose to eat here, and who goes and doesn't.

Why We Go

Condo Tops Go is a small, energetic, and eclectic celebration of Cuban food.

The festival, hosted by the Cinco de Mayo team, is a mix of traditional Cuban food and contemporary Cuban food. Community groups, local street vendors, and local food trucks are all part of the celebration. The local community will gather together, gossip about the food, and take part in the activities.

It's usually in Latin American eateries, but we decided to give it a go here for the best Cuban-Cuban experience possible.

Getting There

We decided to live in a cramped apartment with no kitchen here. We found a good Airbnb and got the Delta Room to live on! The main hub of the event was at the Hilton. Here, we got to check out the other two attractions of the same name, the big rooms, and the small ones.

The Delta Room is on the approach to the hotel. It's not the quickest way to get to the main parking area, but there is always a catch.

Some pictures and video of the festival can be found here.

Hours of Operation:

The festival starts not long after 6pm (we arrived at 6pm) and runs through the night. We eat a certain amount of Cuban food from local vendors.

The St. Patrick's Day holiday is held every year. They also run a runner-up competition: do you have time for a delicious Cuban-Cuban meal or just another short drive away?

You can find out more about picnic plans here. Organizers will give out some and you can pick your favorite service and grab some meat.

We love to hop on flights and get a good view of the Cuban-American community in downtown Havana.

Cinco de Mayo is open for business from 6 to 10 pm.

You can stay at the Cinco de Mayo hotel or in the hotel lobby. Enjoy authentic Cuban food, a meal from Cinco de Mayo, and a great view of Havana.

Amy Brown, who was shot in the back and left leg during a motorcycle fight, is expecting her third child in less than 24 hours.

Investigators say that Amy, an actress and Los Angeles native, went missing about 6:30 p.m. on August 11th.

A witness to the incident says that Amy just pulled her car off the road and ran through a field on a red light. She was shot in the back and left leg in the driver's seat, according to the Los Angeles Police Department.

On August 12th, the women's cell phone was seized and was later recovered by the Ronald McDonald Police Department from the back seat of the car.

Amy's case remains under investigation.

Mr. Lewandowski certainly was a good point of view for the clip, so it's hard to argue he was more than a bit off-putting to the crowd.

The video, which also features audio of him saying it was the first time Mr. Lewandowski had spoken to a woman "in the room," is part of a larger movement to expose Mr. Trump's campaign ties to Russia, which isn't surprising given the allegation that Mr. Lewandowski had worked on an investigation into an alleged pre-dawn drive-by shooting of a Trump campaign staffer.

The clip was first posted online by the Washington Post.

On Twitter, Mr. Lewandowski said, "It is my understanding someone in the other room said something to me that I'm not allowed to talk about in public." Mr. Lewandowski then added, "It was the same as just saying 'f--- Trump.' Said something about virgins. I didn't want to see this."

Another prominent Republican who interviewed reporters in a CNN interview Monday said he found the tension between Mr. Lewandowski and Mr. Trump both to be "addictive."

"It bothers me to hear somebody say, 'I don't like Trump because I'm a misogynist,'" said Robert Gibbs, a Republican candidate for Virginia governor. "I'm not going to go into this right now, but I didn't find it to be insane."

A man who was arrested while fleeing the scene on Tuesday morning and then later charged with aggravated battery on a police officer was still in custody the night before, police said.

Authorities said that 19-year-old Joseph David Ida was arrested in his parked car outside a Burger King at 14th and Jackson streets around 6:30 a.m. Monday. It was unclear if Baumgartner was in the vehicle or if there was an accident as police said they don't know if it involved him.

Ida was taken to the hospital by ambulance. He got back to the Burger King shortly after 8 a.m., police said, and police said he was taken into custody at a hospital and taken to a hospital in the West Village.

According to the arrest warrant, the suspect was arrested, and his name was not released.

Related: Keurig strikes back at new Burger King employee who trolled him

Ida was taken to the hospital where he was later charged with multiple counts of aggravated battery.

Ida is expected to make his first court appearance in the morning at 1 p.m., police said. He will be arraigned at 6 p.m. Tuesday.

A video that appears to show the incident was posted online by the Burger King gang Profile of the Dec. 20 incident:

Deputies said Baumgartner also told them he was a member of the "Fox and Friends" gang.

He was charged with third-degree assault, two counts of malicious wounding and two counts of malicious wounding by a felon, police said.

Ida will now be held in the custody of the Cook County State's Attorney's Office, police said.

According to the warrant, Ida was stopped at the McDonald's across from the Kansas City Police Department and arrested after a deputy saw him speeding 45 mph out of the center of Ingleside, about an hour south of downtown.

Police said Baumgartner left the McDonald's and when he went inside, his car was taken by police — and now he is in the custody of the Cook County State's Attorney's Office.

Authorities said they had "no motive" to stop his car, which is off Monroe Avenue and has no sign of a deputy or officer, from the scene.

Last year, a University of Queensland researcher wanted to evaluate the long-term effects of cannabis on the brain.

While the research focused on ASD and ADHD, it presented a new group of researchers but it was a small group, which highlights the need for further investigation.

In the interest of creating a way for researchers to capture and monitor the effects of cannabis on the brain, a Polish team is pursuing a method for monitoring the effects of cannabis in Australia and New Zealand.

They are using a combination of new methodologies, such as Olfactory Plasticity and Sensorimotor Activity Measures, which assess how cannabis has changed the brain.

Olfactory plasticity measures the number of times the brain changes between different phases of the functioning. An MPI is a measure of the number of times a person can place a button if given a certain amount of cannabis.

Potential side effects include loss of sexual pleasure, abnormal motor rhythm and increased aggression, behaviour impairment and irritability.

Fifty-four of the researchers using the new method, including four of those from Poland, reported strong changes in the brain changes during cannabis use.

The team said after studying the data for two years, they discovered that the brain changes were actually caused within the next two years.

They say that the effects are more severe for children aged between one and three years.

"We were very surprised to find that the age-specific changes were significant, but in fact, they were only seen in children with an age range of 1 to 5 years," Dr. Donnelly said.

The study comes with the caveat that these small studies have been limited.

"It will be interesting to find if these differences will translate to measurable long-term changes, but for now we are not sure whether that translates into completely lasting effects."

Dr Donnelly said that the results showed that the enhanced effects are one of the major areas of research that needs to be dated and mature.

In a previous study, a team from the University of Queensland, Queensland, and the University of Washington in Seattle, Washington, used MRI to measure brain activity.

In this study the researchers measured brain activity in 20 participants aged between 1 and 7 years and 20,000 participants.

Results showed that the left and right hemisphere of the brain were less active during cannabis use in the participants who were also college students.

Dr Donnelly said that although alcohol use was associated with increased brain activity, the two types of cannabis were not.

"We saw several possible explanations for the decreased activity in the right hemisphere, of course, but we theorised that the brain's concentration compared to the left might have been a measure of its action on an even playing field," he said.

"The events of light and darkness were at odds with each other, but the intensity of the events on the right side of the brain does not correspond to the activity of the left hemisphere."

This is at odds with the research the research mentioned in the book.

Cannabis is regarded as a safe alternative to alcohol and schizophrenia for some people and for others, they said.

"Cannabis use is common in Australia and in many other developed countries, especially in OECD countries where adults are still extremely young and in many communities," said Dr Donnelly.

"For our purposes, we focused on setting up a laboratory setting.

"We also have validation studies in the US and Europe to assess the impact of cannabis on some aspects of behaviour."

Dr Donnelly said that one of the key areas of research for the future is to understand how cannabis affects the brain while using cannabis.

"We are hoping that we will be able to make a better understanding of the link between cannabis use and schizophrenia," he said.

"For example, on the street, we might be able to check for patterns of marijuana use inside of a person's body, and see if cannabis alters brain chemistry and learning.

"This could have an impact on some of the risks of cannabis use, and also on the size of the C-Suite that we would like to build in the future."

One of the many hidden gems of the Golden Age of video game journalism was the fact that the 'Game of Thrones' characters worked so well. Perhaps they weren't the best as major players in Westeros and the whole of the world. Perhaps they were the sweet spot for the characters who made it to the big screen and the places where they did so much good! But there was at least one character, a Wolf's Dog, who was an enormous success.

The King is a bitch

This wasn't just due to his vicious nature. This was just something that was very well done and had to be done badly. One of the best things about Jon Snow was the fact that he seemed capable of handling anything in his life. Though I felt a little weird at first watching him at the end of Season 3. I felt like I was just a little bit stuck and after trying for a while I realised that he was capable of everything and was going to be great.

Royalty is a bitch

The fact that Tyrion was good at holding his own even against his political opponents helped you feel more entitled. Tyrion was a man of the people. He had a lot of freedom with his life and was able to handle what he wanted without any big problems. I felt that he had a lot of privilege in his life and couldn't handle anything he wanted. Tyrion was able to enjoy the life he had started and was able to handle things that he had not had any time for.

When presented with this fact of his, who would he be as King and what kind of role he would play? Of course, Tyrion knew that he had no choice but to act in a very dangerous or dangerous situation. It was horrible to hear this as we saw Young Caster Tyrion acting as a character who had the potential to become something. King do you know what I mean? Which would you rather be?

This is why I LOVE the story aspect of bestiality. This would hardly be a new thing, but it's something that the show just was.

Because of this, I love the characters

Youth takes his turn

I loved this moment when Tyrion told Tyrion that he was going to the slaughter and that he could actually do something. He knew he was going to win. Tyrion is a great center of gravity in this show and it was something I wanted to see return to, but thanks to this decision I could see myself not being around. Once the characters introduced Tyrion that was what I wanted to see a lot more of.

With the Hubble Space Telescope, astronomers are using its immense power – especially for the first time – to study the size of invisible matter in the universe.

This gives astronomers the ability to view the location of invisible matter around the larger universe.

Scientists are also using Hubble to look for new, dark particles. This allows us to access what scientists call the "dark matter hole," which is where the light from the inner black hole, called a supermassive black hole, ends up.

"We've been able to see this dark matter hole for the first time in our galaxy, and that means we have a much better idea of what we're looking for out there in the cosmos," said Starslick.

While Hubble and other telescopes have given us more information about the universe, this time, astronomers are contributing to our understanding because they are using data from other telescopes to study the dark matter hole.

"The newer telescopes are better at seeing the dark matter, less could they be," said Michael Pillsbury, an astrophysicist at the University of Connecticut who was not involved in the study. "But if you take over 17 years, and look at the bigger picture, you can tell that the only way to ever find that dark matter hole in the universe is to find black holes."

Astronomers say by looking at the dark matter hole, they are also helping us learn more about the universe.

"The more we understand the universe, the more we can tell that there is a dark matter hole we can't see," said professor of astronomy Michael Carlin, a senior author of the Hubble Space Telescope's 16-minute video talk.

The new study was funded by the National Science Foundation.

More information about this research can be found at the Hubble Space Telescope web site.

The backstop is a small structure that provides one of the best locations of an ATV, or long way out of the way. The device is balanced so that the leaf swing is always on the ground. The front end changes positions to accommodate more power to the rear end (collectors and set-ups).

The front end is mounted inside a single rear end, which allows room for more power to be received from the rear end. It is mounted on the front end or rear end of the ATV. The rear end is located with a magnet attachment from the base to the base of the front end (making it easy to focus).

This device provides several functions.

Works seamlessly with ATV. This daily power is the same as it is on full auto.

Allows you to load a battery or other items onto the backstop.

Lightweight and easy to carry. The ATV does not require any additional care. It's wide open.

Battery is aluminized, meaning it can hold up to 6 hours of battery life.

50V can be charged and discharged in no time.

The backside of the device provides enough power for the ATV.

Like all ATV's, the backstop comes with a 2.5" piece of brass for keeping it from scratching or rusting. This piece consists of a proprietary metal sponge, which is made from stainless steel.

The pouch is a thin plastic shell that's about 35" long and 45" wide. To prevent scratches, the plastic pouch will not slip off.

The configuration does not allow the front end to tilt, so you have to take special care to secure the back end into the back of the device.

The backends in the front end are designed for use in a variety of situations and needs. We have tested various ATV's for more than 100 years in service.

The top of the device is made out of hand-sealed V3 titanium, which is an extremely soft material that's extremely durable. This rock-hard hard titanium is made of 10,000-pounds of material. It is waterproof and corrosion-resistant, even with temperatures between 65 degrees F to 69 degrees F. It plugs to the open end and provides easy access for the front end.

You can store up to 150 lbs. of ATV equipment inside the device.

The back of the device transfers the power directly from the front end. The front end is allowed to rotate in and out of the device. ATV's use the power from the front end, and it does it in a robust and dependable fashion.

.@Noam Chomsky: "We must now bring up terrorism without condemning it." https://t.co/cxIx1oO8jN pic.twitter.com/nzHTrvQmYz — New York Times (@nytimes) February 24, 2016

That's not to say that we must not be more militant. The problem is that to do so is to align ourselves with a system that broadly approves of extremism.

But we should also take a stand.

Our First Amendment is free speech, but it's not sufficient to say that Sharia should be banned or that our nation should eventually ban American Muslims.

This is not to say we have to enforce the First Amendment right to stifle speech or even silence others. Rather, we must protect it in the name of freedom of expression.

We can't simply ignore the threat of terrorism because it is not our job to ban Islam.

The problem comes when we play the role of the leader of the free world.

This is the role democracy and tolerance play.

So why are we afraid of amplification?

There are many ways to avoid fear.

We can "like-minded" democracy. We can participate in (any) democratic society.

We can accept criticism as evidence based on facts. Or we may just tolerate silence.

Or we may think we may be "under attack" from what we consider to be the same enemies called Islamic State.

Or we may simply be willing to tolerate a foreign terrorist group that is, in effect, attacking our country.

Or we may simply accept it as a fact that terrorist groups and their supporters are in Europe.

Dennis Rodman was a rabid defender of the First Amendment in his defense of our way of life.

If you think it's OK to be critical of people like Rodman and other terrorists, then you are a victim (and a potential terrorist).

Yet when it comes to the question of whether people like Rodman and other terrorists are engaged in terrorism, we fear that we ignore our own free speech.

Why?

We are afraid of amplification. We are alarmed about threats. And we are frightened of being heard.

Consider what you have seen from prominent journalists these past few weeks.

A Washington Post investigative journalist named James Risen, for instance, revealed today that he had been approached by a prominent Islamic extremist named Abu Saeed al-Mashaloui, who was detained in September and brought to the U.S. for interrogation by his interrogators last year.

He told Al Jazeera that Abu Saeed's tactics could allow for "credible commandos to surround you and extract your confessions. And then you can rape your way out of your cell, and if you're successful, you can be released. And if you're an extremist, you can be a terrorist."

The Times story went on to express this fear:

As public outrage grew in the wake of the May 2015 massacre at a U.S.-based Muslim tourist center in Orlando, such information was sent through a network of Islamic militants, who were monitoring the site and commandeering people's phones, Internet cafes and contact lists.

It was not until this week that the United States authorities announced that they would begin to disclose information about the American Muhannad Mujahideen group, led by a senior Islamic activist called Abu Mohammed al-Britani, that seemed to be following the Muslim Brotherhood … until now.

A cop on a disabled officer was shot dead in Lincoln County on Monday, November 17.

According to the Department of Public Safety, 51-year-old Jeffrey Holstad opened fire on a patrolman near Myrtle Beach, South Carolina, at around 12:45 p.m. when he saw a man with a gun and refused him a drink, according to the Times Union.

"There was no conversation with him at that time," the officer said. "If anyone asked for a drink, you'd be called to the scene. He was a good guy. He did not get a drink. He was just a good guy. Up until this point he was a pretty mellow guy who wouldn't get out of his car and just run around. His demeanor was very calm and was friendly. And he was not fighting with his life."

Myrtle Beach Police Chief Timothy Curley said the cop was shot multiple times in the chest during the shooting, and is in stable condition at John F. Kennedy Medical Center. He did not name a cause of death.

Neighbors told the Times Union that Holstad, who had a history of mental illness and practices yoga or meditation, was a quiet, cheerful guy.

"He didn't do any violent stuff," said Gary Hayes, who lives across the street from Holstad. "He was just a nice guy."

"He was just a good guy. He was just a nice guy," added neighbor Matt Morse, who said he saw Holstad in a park nearby on Monday. "He put his hands up and said 'Stop shooting. Stop shooting.' That's what he did."

"He was kind of a negative influence to everybody," said neighbor Tim Hutchings, who also lives across the street. "He was a character around here, but he was kind of a nice guy."

Holstad was one of four officers charged in the fatal shooting. The other three officers were arrested on drug charges, and one was released on $25,000 bail.

A News4 file photo shows Jeffrey Holstad in handcuffs during a protest against racial violence. (News4) (News4)

A Lincoln County grand jury indicted three of the officers on charges of first-degree murder for the officer's killing. A rioting suspect was arrested on suspicion of rioting, while another was subsequently charged with assault and battery on a law enforcement officer, police said.

It is the second case for the death of a police officer in Lincoln County since April 17.

Four officers were taken to the hospital with gunshot wounds after a May 6 fire that left five officers dead.

Four officers were indicted on multiple charges, including first-degree murder, conspiracy, kidnapping and attempted murder, police said. The other three were released on $25,000 bail.

Holstad's double murder case is the latest controversy in a series of police killings involving disabled police officers across the state.

In July, responding police officers were shot after they left their patrol cars on the side of a highway in East Newport, Rhode Island.

Northampton County Councilman Pat Regan, who represents the area, said he is pleased that the changes in the state law are making things easier for disabled police officers.

"The way I see the laws are changing, if it saves lives, then I don't see it as something that's going to get me anywhere," Regan said. "I understand there is some concern that we might end up becoming more of a place where people have to fight for their lives and be able to protect themselves. I think we're seeing a certain strain where they don't have any protection against this type of violence."

The Associated Press contributed to this report.

"It's got to be something special," Rose said. "There's no doubt about it. They've been great to me."

Rose is now a top-15 pick after averaging 9.0 points, 6.2 rebounds and 5.8 assists when healthy during his three years with the Green Bay Packers.

He first battled the symptoms of his illness in 2008, when he suffered a hip injury while playing in the studio of ESPN. After missing three months in the 2014 preseason, Rose returned to play in the NFL, sustaining a separate hip injury.

"He's still here, he can play his game," Green Bay coach Mike McCarthy said. "He's an incredible young guy."

Rose held down the starting spot for the Packers, playing 60 percent of their preseason games and helping them to a point streak of 22 consecutive games without an interception.

The Packers led by as much as 19 points on the second play of the third quarter, when they had the ball with a 40-yard scoring run with 1:36 left to play. It was the first time since 2002 that the Packers had six turnovers or more in a game.

"It was unbelievable," Rose said. "He's a great guy."

After an injury to a knee suffered in Week 3 gave Green Bay a chance to win, Rose had seven stops, including five second-quarter stops. He was one of only five players to record five or more points during a single game.

He performed well in the second half, and he went on to score 11 points, though he only had two field goals.

The Packers scored three more points when they had a chance to play a two-point lead on a 38-yard pass that went 40 yards downfield, but they went with a 1-yard pass from Paul Guandolo, then juked into the end zone.

It was the fourth time in six games that the Packers had four or fewer field goals in a game for an important third quarter, and three times they had them in game six by halftime.

"I just wanted to make sure we kept throwing the ball in," Rose said. "It was just a great way for me to take the ball down field. I'm just glad I was able to play."

The Green Bay offense did not do much good against Rose, who has one of the league's highest offensive rebounding percentages, and he is averaging 3.5 yards per carry despite his initial struggles.

"He's a very younger player," said center Matt Flynn, who has started all 10 games this season. "He has really grown since he came out of the hospital. He's just more reflective. He's just a much more established player, especially now. He came out of it trying to make a change. It was crazy and unbelievable."

The Packers did not have an NBA superstar the rest of the way, but Rose had a monster game for the first time since 2010 against Cleveland in Minnesota. He had 33 points and 13 rebounds while shooting 27.1 percent from the field while scoring 10 of his 19 points in the third quarter.

Asked if he was disappointed with his rookie slump, Rose said: "I'm not trying to pander to anybody. He's just trying to go out there and play. He's just doing the right thing. He's just focused on winning."

Rose, who had four turnovers in a loss to the New Orleans Pelicans on Thursday, led the Packers with 14 points and 12 rebounds. His average of 5.1 points was half his career low.

The Packers are outrebounded 72-28 by their opponents after openers, and they have allowed just four points in their last 19 away from Lambeau Field.

As an adult, I're not a social animal. I'd like to think that a person's development is about whether they're ready for maturity. But I've never been physically mature, even though I'm called a kid. While I don't always have a problem with someone wearing clothes that look a little too young, it's always the same thing. I wish I was a little more mature, but I'm not, I'm still a little bit older than I was. I'm still a little bit old, but I'm still learning.

With your rocker, Tower of Fire, you spoke about the sound you can hear when you dance and your gender identity, and you talked about one thing you like to say about yourself. Do you think you're a lucky person?

I'm lucky. Maybe I'm looking for joy in my life, but that's not what I want to see. I'm searching for happiness that I'm not looking for or anything like that. I don't know if that's because I was living my life in a place where I didn't feel comfortable, or maybe if it's because I'm still very young, or whatever it is, I don't know.

What's your favorite song?

I can't say. I love that song, but I think it's a bit out of place. If anyone wants to dance, they'll have to do it in the restroom. It's not the way I was or what I'm supposed to look like, so I don't have much of a choice. It's just one of those things.

What song do you like to sing?

It sounds very laid back, but if I was 12, I'd probably want to sing "I'm not a sing-a-long" or "I'm a little bit disappointed" or something like that. That's my favorite song, actually.

But if you were 19, you could have performed a whole album.

I don't think I could have. I'm a pretty young person, really. I think I'm pretty terrible at figuring out what I was thinking. I guess I'm just trying to figure out what I'm feeling. I don't have the white hair and I don't have this white face. I don't have the hair that I'm wearing now in a bunch of different spots and I don't have the hair that I'm wearing now in my sis. I guess I guess I guess I'm getting older and I just don't think I could ever grow up like that. But I'm probably only six now.

Do you think you can get into a modeling career?

No. The only thing I can do at the moment is talk to other people about myself. I want to start doing something now, so I don't have to worry about that. I'm one of those people who just wants people to understand that I'm a pretty ill-advised person. I would be very willing to go to a porn star, but I don't have that much control over that. I would be a good guy.

What are some of your goals as a young adult? Do you smoke cigarettes, or do you stay away from alcohol?

I've never like to smoke. I smoke cigarettes.

What's your favorite restaurant?

The Kitchen Break. It's one of my favorites because it's like a restaurant where I can order it and it's from my mom. I could be at something, but it's kind of like the restaurant where you take your kids and they're going to go eat there. I've never been at a restaurant like that before, and it's really just a really chill place, and it's just kind of a quiet place. The thing is, I've never really been into any food or anything. I'm just pretty good at eating, and I'm pretty good at watching a movie or anything. When I'm not busy, I don't really have much to look forward to.

You're so old that you're embarrassed by things you're not able to tell, and the more you think about them, the more worn out you become. Why do you feel embarrassed?

I think it's kind of weird that I can feel myself. I try to let everything go, but I love to take things about myself that don't really warrant any attention, and that I can relate to. It's hard to pull off when you're the youngest, but I've had so many people come to me, and I've had so many people using their relationship as a thing to show me how amazing I am. Having to do this kind of thing, I think I've been blessed with so much. I think that's why I go to the movies, because I want the best.

I grew up in a family of ...

I'm not sure what they were talking about, but...

I don't know.

Many of these bonds with others, most notably for NAFTA, the United States Postal Service, the Postal Service, and the Department of Labor, were in dispute in the 1990s and were weakened by the recession. The bankruptcy of the PSRA and the recession of the United States Postal Service have also long fueled the growth and growth of mortgage interest rates.

While the Bankruptcy Act of 1982 allowed the government to "reduce" to what would have been $2 trillion in debt a debt restructuring that would have been left to others, the law also required the Fed to take action in the event of bankruptcy. With the recession of 2009 and the decision to repeatedly raise interest rates, debts could get even more expensive as a result of increased interest rate competition. The Dodd-Frank financial reform law, which took effect in July 2010, has opened up a new set of possibilities for government to raise rates. The Federal Reserve has also been exploring the possibility of increasing the size of the government, tapping into existing deposits of US government money to pay for the services of government agents.

Conventional wisdom states that the Federal Reserve's powers over these powers are socially controversial. Many believe the Fed is merely a glorified bank. And it is. But for many in the business community it is even more controversial.

Yet, it's not just the Fed that is seemingly spent on mortgage interest rates. Critics, however, point out that the ECB is a burden on the economy, champions of monetary policy, and is also a source of immense emotional and political power. The highest federal official in the world, the ECB, has an estimated $23 billion in assets. A private equity firm, Citigroup, has $21 billion in liabilities. The Bank of England has $15 billion, and the government's Reserve Bank of India has $10 billion. And for all the talk about the Fed being "in charge", it's not.

Sources, References, and Further Reading

Georges Flemming, "Consciousness, Accountability, and the Federal Reserve's Role", In Financial Markets and the Federal Reserve: A Critical History (New York: Basic Books, 2000), p. 22.

E. Michael Paulson, "Fed," Iconomics, Vol. 35, No. 3 April 2006, pp. 41-55.

Most of those who could be considered targets in the future but have been pulled from the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species Act (CITES) last year will be taken by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID), which, as per its agreement with the US government, will direct the following actions:

To expedite implementation of the 2016 Agreement

In order to expedite the implementation of the 2016 Agreement, for example, the Department of State has agreed to provide a list of excluded species, including the most up-to-date listed species (including non-UN listed species such as bison, buffalo, bears, leopards and wolves).

There are currently no changes to the State Department's decision to appoint the individuals that are allegedly bringing about an end to the Convention and to replace them with individuals who have been in violation of the Convention, including some of the most common illegal wildlife species (including bison, buffalo, bears, leopards, wolves and coyotes).

As of March 31, 2016, the stated objectives of the Agreement were to make the country in the Pacific a fully member of the Convention (including the United States, Australia, Brunei Darussalam, Chile, Costa Rica, Chile, Chile, El Salvador, Dagestan, Finland, France, Germany, Guatemala, Grenada, Hungary, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Philippines, New Zealand, Paraguay, Peru, Turkey, and Vietnam).

The Agreement also included proposals to increase the United States' responsibilities in the international community in effecting the Convention – including in the form of personnel, goods, and facilities.

Start Date: September 30, 2016

End Date: September 30, 2016

This agreement includes deal making for the USAID's implementation.

I recently upgraded to the new GS5, which got me a little more room to myself, but I didn't think much of it until I listened to the audio.

The sounds are the same as the original GS5, with slightly different ones. Love it sound and feel - really like the original. But the feel is still the same, with new keys...

The GS5 also has an MP3 player, and also a USB OTG port. You can also connect this to your PC via USB, as well as SATA drives and Ethernet cards.

The app itself is fairly simple to use. Having a basic PC music player, a list of songs to play, and the ability to get home music and music apps up and running is just about as useful as using a smartphone.

(If you have an iPod Touch, you can easily install the app from here, as well as other apps on the Play Store.)

Note that the above video is a bit of an event. The game is actually harsh on the rabbit, so some things aren't as good as others.

(Note on "Move Over" in the Amazon Appstore: I was expecting more on the basics, but with the app, it was like using a video monitor.)

The app is very easy to use, and it is also very similar to the Sony Music Store. It's also currently starting up in the new Google Play store, which is pretty cool.

A few things you'll notice about the app are that it is very easy to navigate, so you can get songs and music apps to work at will.

To answer your question, yes you can move a current song in the playlist.

I bought the G5's from Amazon.

The app for the G5 comes with a pre-built SDR Audio 5.1 kit that lets you download songs from Google Play, and I had the opportunity to use it with the original, but it does take a little longer to load, and I have to actually unplug the case.

A couple of things have changed...

The 5.1 Kit does not include the built-in GPS system that Google recommends, or the built-in Track pad that allows for some stuff like the Play Store to store your music, so you can't rely on Google Voice.

The phone doesn't have a headphone jack, so out of the box, the app works just fine.

While not as powerful as the GS5's, the 6.0-inch screen is nice in this respect: the app is responsive, looks great, and it looks an awful lot better than the G5.

The app app is split into two parts: the music main menu and the main navigation.

The main menu is where your music plays, which is the big thing here. On the G5, if the navigation is mixed with the main menu you will get an error message about not being able to play your music.

The navigation is divided into a shorter sections, called sections. If you want to play a song, but you have a separate section to play it from, to the left of the main navigation section, you just walk up off the main navigation section to go back.

When you flip to the side of the main navigation section, the main navigation doesn't play, but you can flip to the right and play the song you want.

Scanning the main navigation section is a little old-fashioned as well. There are only two rows of navigation.

There is no tracking to allow you to pick up the main navigation, so you have to drag and drop around these tiles (or some other device's parallax sensor) to see what the song is.

The G5's main navigation is actually a bit more difficult to use, especially on a laptop, and when I tried it with a smartwatch, it showed me the main navigation didn't work at all.

Some people are awed by the new app, which is what you see in the screenshots. I think it's pretty cool, and I'll continue to play this on my smartwatch, but I'm actually not paid to use it.

The current list of apps I'm having issues with is the ones you'll need to set everything up for your music store to work.

Boasting a whopping 410,000 more processing units per year, the company says the country is poised to get 35% of the global market by 2020. It also predicts the country will become the second to overtake China in the 1990s and '00s with 70% of the global demand by 2025.

The country's gross domestic product growth rate in 2015 was estimated at output per capita of more than 2.25% on the global market. Japan's GDP growth rate is estimated at 1.5% and South Korea's benchmark GDP growth rate of 7.2% it said.

China has issued record-breaking bonds to manage its debt-ridden economy and ambitious economic growth plan has dismayed analysts.

"China's recent record-breaking bond issuance and the data reflecting this are a reminder that the real economy is changing as a result of China's unprecedented debt restructurings," said Boon Li, head of S&P Global Ratings. "The economy needs to adapt to the new paradigm every time a currency devalues, which will reduce the value of the yuan, as well as increase the need for debt restructuring."

S&P also expects the country to grow at a 33% pace over the next five years, taking significant measures to counteract the country's falling Chinese borrowing costs because the country is expected to close its fiscal deficit by 2020.

In a separate report, data on exchanges and trade after the country's July 2008 financial crisis also predicted a decline in the number of public-sector jobs.

However, experts have warned that putting prices down is not the answer, particularly given that China is also making gains from its debt to the tune of 10% of gross domestic product and 5.3% of its GDP growth.

"We think this reflects the social and political pressure on the politicians, and we will continue to push to stay in place and to focus if that is too much and could put us in a situation where we will not be able to handle our debts," said Young Chang, economist at BNP Paribas who has been at Moody's Asian Ratings Group since 2011.

The banking crisis in 2008, in which the government imposed a two-year moratorium on official accounts, exposed China's growing size and scale.

By pickering off its debt burden, China was able to finance economic growth at a rapid rate, which is providing the impetus for the country's post-crisis economic boom.

Analysts say Teng Kuo, the head of China's central bank, has said such economic growth could weaken as corporate profits decline and the government's decision to make capital controls more aggressive helped to accelerate growth.

"China's real GDP growth may stall further well into the next generation," Kuo told MEE in an interview. "But we believe there is an upside for the economy, especially in investment, as the the Chinese economy will be able to expand again for the first time since the 2008-09 financial crisis."

In just twenty-two years, South African history will be remembered as an era of subversion against a backdrop of international peace. The demonstrations and riots that followed, culminating in the 1973 national liberation of South Africa, have destroyed South Africa and destroyed the power of the Sahel. These are largely new facts.

Although South Africa continues to be part of the world's largest economy, the state has little hope of ever recovering from the consequences of its actions, and that hope remains the only hope left with South Africa. The South African government has erected a brilliant policy of racial integration, intent on ensuring that the people of South Africa are given the assurance and help they need as they adjust to an increasingly multicultural and social world. It has embraced a more liberal and democratic approach, and no other country has such a strong and consistent policy of racial integration.

But it is not to be overstated that South Africa is not a far-away dream. The struggle of the African people for protection against a future immigration system that assumes a world of racial apartheid is already underway. It is occurring, and is helping create real problems.

A new South African government is needed. It will set the stage for action and create real solutions. It will be in charge of a country that will change. The country needs a national leader of open and honest political will that will stand up for the rights of all Africans.

A struggle will begin with the important task of making sure that racial apartheid is abolished. The only way to end racial apartheid is through an effective international dialogue. It is a necessary first step. And it is the only way that we can end racial apartheid in the South African political system.

As we approach 23rd century world history, we look at the key moment in our history to mark the beginning of a new era in which humanity, free from racism, will start to live free of injustice and exploitation. In that moment, we are faced with an imperative that is urgent: the right to self-determination.

Make sense of this moment?

On Sunday night, Republican House Speaker Paul Ryan of Wisconsin made a statement to the nation about the Republican House leadership.

"For as long as I can remember," Ryan said, "we have been a Republican Party that protects every American's Second Amendment rights and no one denies that."

Ryan was referring to the latest National Defense Authorization Act that includes a provision that endorses local government over any other branch of government.

The House Republican leadership has repeatedly refused to release details of Ryan's statement, and the White House has issued a statement saying, "Republicans are well aware that we need to keep our leaders in line."


Ryan's statement was accompanied by a video that was released by the Congressional Budget Office on Sunday evening. The video added that, "The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) found that 24 million Americans would lose the right to buy firearms and assault weapons from the federal government in 10 years" if Congress only increased funding for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) by $7.6 billion.

The video stretched to 1.3 million views per minute.

"I don't think this debate should be about the Senate or the House," Ryan said. "It should be about the members of Congress and the American people."

As Fox News host Sean Hannity stood by Ryan and his comments, Dan Zuckerman, a senior fellow for education at the Roosevelt Institute, questioned what Ryan meant by the language in the NDAA which includes the provision that was previously known as the "National Defense Authorization Act."

Zuckerman then asked aloud the question of whether if Ryan meant this language even though it was in his budget, the individual House members who would be affected would be able to keep their original law.
